commands.repair_reason: - [P2] There is a narrow mechanical repair: make the trusted-proxy proof reject mixed broad proxy source lists and add one focused regression test.; Needs attention: The diff still has a concrete security-diagnostic gap for mixed trusted proxy source lists.; Review finding: [P1] Reject mixed broad trusted-proxy entries before downgrading — src/commands/doctor-security.ts:144 (sha=7a8a6e0dc1578c4af7d6f7b6bfa179e377d76041)
commands.review_summary:
The branch updates doctor security diagnostics so complete trusted-proxy gateway auth is reported as an exposed-auth warning instead of unauthenticated critical, with regression coverage for the happy path and several invalid trusted-proxy cases.

PR surface: Source +110, Tests +127. Total +237 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main's doctor exposure check only treats token/password shared secrets as auth proof, while runtime and docs define trusted-proxy as a gateway auth mode. I did not run the CLI because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is a narrow mechanical repair: make the trusted-proxy proof reject mixed broad proxy source lists and add one focused regression test.

target.title: fix: treat trusted-proxy gateway auth as doctor auth proof
target.body:
## Summary

- Count a fully configured `trusted-proxy` gateway setup as authentication proof for the network-exposure doctor check.
- Keep incomplete trusted-proxy setups on the existing critical path by requiring both `gateway.auth.trustedProxy.userHeader` and `gateway.trustedProxies`.
- Keep startup-invalid trusted-proxy/token conflicts and unsafe proxy source configs on specific critical paths.
- Replace the misleading token/`doctor --fix` remediation with trusted-proxy-specific proxy/firewall guidance.

## Why

`trusted-proxy` is an explicit gateway auth mode, and the gateway runtime already fails closed when the trusted proxy settings or `gateway.trustedProxies` list are missing. The doctor exposure check only recognized token/password shared-secret modes, so a LAN-bound trusted-proxy deployment could be reported as "without authentication" and nudged toward token repair even though the intended auth boundary is the reverse proxy.

This keeps the exposure warning, because a LAN-bound gateway still deserves review, but it no longer labels a complete trusted-proxy setup as unauthenticated.

It also avoids treating unsafe or startup-invalid trusted-proxy configs as proof: shared-token conflicts, broad proxy CIDRs/default routes, and loopback proxy entries without explicit `allowLoopback` remain critical.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: LAN-bound Gateway configs using fully configured `trusted-proxy` auth were reported as unauthenticated by doctor/security.
- Real environment tested: A real local OpenClaw config using `gateway.bind=lan`, `gateway.auth.mode=trusted-proxy`, and configured trusted proxies.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `npm_config_cache=/private/tmp/openclaw-npm-cache npm exec --yes pnpm@11.2.2 -- node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json`
- Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the patched dev CLI included these `core/doctor/security` findings:

```json
[
  {
    "checkId": "core/doctor/security",
    "severity": "warning",
    "message": "WARNING: Gateway bound to \"lan\" (0.0.0.0) with trusted-proxy authentication."
  },
  {
    "checkId": "core/doctor/security",
    "severity": "warning",
    "message": "Ensure only configured trusted proxies can reach the Gateway port; block direct clients at a host firewall, network firewall, or loopback bind."
  },
  {
    "checkId": "core/doctor/security",
    "severity": "warning",
    "message": "Docs: https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/trusted-proxy-auth"
  }
]
```

- Observed result after fix: The trusted-proxy Gateway config is no longer reported as `CRITICAL` or "without authentication"; doctor keeps a network-exposure warning with trusted-proxy-specific guidance.
- What was not tested: I did not test a live remote reverse-proxy login flow from this branch; the proof is limited to the doctor/security classification and remediation text.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-security.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-security.ts src/commands/doctor-security.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
